textPlain: Two oversights in this article:- Failure to mention Netscape Enterprise Server (NodeJS is not responsible for expanding "the language's scope[…] far beyond the browser"—it was on the server from almost the very beginning; the author cites Brendan's 2011 blog post[1] which namechecks Rhino, but then leaves this out)- Failure to mention JS running on the James Webb Space Telescope (Brendan's post also namechecks Nombas, but doesn't go into much detail about it; Brent Noorda covered this in an update to the Nombas section of his site[2] in 2022)1. <https://brendaneich.com/2011/06/>2. <https://brent-noorda.com/nombas/us/index.htm>

Brendan Eich wanted the language be some dialect of  Lisp language but his superiors insisted on a language similar to Java. So he wrote that prototype in Common Lisp. It's basically Java like syntax over Common Lisp structs and vectors.
Still have this in my bookmarks file:http://devedge.netscape.com/library/manuals/2000/javascript/...
